Label  P (torr)  I (amp)  d (nm)  σ  a (Å)
Acute inflammation is a complex biological response that can significantly impact various physiological processes. In the
context of this study, we investigate the effects of PI3Kγ deficiency on inflammatory responses, utilizing several metrics to
quantify the underlying biological mechanisms. The data presented in the accompanying table compares the responses of wild-
type (wt) and PI3Kγ-/- samples across multiple parameters relevant to inflammation. Notably, the columns labeled "P (torr)"
and "I (amp)" provide critical insights into the pressure and current measurements associated with the inflammatory response

under varying conditions. 

The results indicate a variation in measured values, with wt samples exhibiting a P (torr) reading of
3.0, reflecting the physiological alterations that occur during acute inflammation. Conversely, the PI3Kγ-/- samples display
distinct differences in their I (amp) readings, which may correlate with the underlying molecular mechanisms activated in
response to inflammation. Understanding these differences is essential for elucidating the role of PI3Kγ in modulating
inflammatory pathways and could have significant implications for therapeutic strategies targeting inflammatory diseases. This
data underscores the importance of precise measurements in characterizing the inflammatory response and highlights the need
for further investigation in this area.
